title: Annona helosioides
taxonomy: Annona helosioides is classified in the family Annonaceae, commonly known as the custard apple family. This family consists of over 130 genera and approximately 2,000 species. The genus Annona includes around 130 species of flowering plants, many of which are edible and produce fruits such as custard apples and cherimoyas. Annona helosioides is a species of evergreen tree that is native to Central and South America. Its classification is also sometimes placed in the subfamily Annoneae within the family Annonaceae.
uses: Annona helosioides is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. Traditionally, it has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions. The plant's roots and leaves are rich in bioactive compounds, which may contribute to its medicinal effects. Some research suggests that Annona helosioides may have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. However, further studies are needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential health benefits.
